images.caption: Figure 1:MIM in Different Paradigms.(a) Self-Supervised Learning: Employs high-ratio masking as a self-supervised proxy task for representation learning. (b) Generative Tokenizers: A two-stage approach where the latent space is pre-trained with MIM before training a separate generative model. (c) MIMFlow (Ours): A unified framework that jointly optimizes latent semantics, pixel reconstruction, and generative flow in an end-to-end manner.
Figure 2:Structure of MIMFlow.NNis the number of image patches,KKis the number of learnable latent query tokens,𝐦\mathbf{m}is the binary mask, andeedenotes learnable decoder embeddings.
Figure 3:Selected Sampleson ImageNet 256 × 256 from MIMFlow-L. We use classifier-free guidance equal to 2.0.

Figure 5:Jacobian Spectral Analysisof STARFlow and MIMFlow. The three panels report, from left to right, the empirical distributions of the largest singular valueσmax​(J)\sigma_{\max}(J), the smallest singular valueσmin​(J)\sigma_{\min}(J), and the log-condition numberlog10⁡κ​(J)\log_{10}\kappa(J)(withκ​(J)=σmax​(J)/σmin​(J)\kappa(J)=\sigma_{\max}(J)/\sigma_{\min}(J)).
Figure 6:Linear Probe Accuracy vs Depthunder Different Mask Ratios.
